evidencePolicy: Public export keeps only evidence paths inside public trust packages/docs/contracts. Private app, server, ops, generated artifact, and credential-bearing evidence paths are counted but not listed.

gates.requirement: Escrow draft must match BOX-only internal-balance Session escrow model.

Escrow unit tests must cover exact BOX transfer accounting, deposit, lock, settlement conservation, payload-bound settlement id, profit fee, replay rejection, self-service withdrawal/claim/refund, manual-review, pause controls, fee notice, fee cap, and permission boundaries.
Backend/relayer/escrow integration tests must validate shared settlement fixtures against the contract-adjacent flow.

BSC source verification must be completed for the deployed escrow contract, and post-deploy evidence must show matching source, constructor args, bytecode, owner, relayer, treasury, fee params, and BSCScan readback.
Independent contract review or audit notes must be available, and critical findings must be remediated or explicitly accepted before real BOX rooms.
Game-core and backend transcript/replay fixtures must pass.
Production-like Real BOX rooms must reject mock action envelopes and unverified DeBox action envelopes while keeping ordinary bid/play/pass low-friction through server-verified player sessions.
Server dealing, first-bidder selection, public replay helpers, and replay fixtures must use the same game-core round-deal derivation function.
Every real-money round must use a server shuffle nonce committed before any player ready nonce, then reveal and validate that commitment before dealing.
Unfunded real-money create/join seats must not reserve a wallet address globally or block the real wallet owner from entering another room.
HTTP and realtime endpoints must enforce body, payload, message-rate, and subscription limits so malformed or excessive traffic fails without taking down active tables.
Player-facing room HTTP and realtime views must not expose opponent private hands.
Frontend money-flow acceptance checks must pass in DeBox production-like runtime.
Deployment, rollback, relayer, monitoring, support, and emergency procedures must be documented.
V1 classic operations monitoring must cover settlement delay, relayer balance, RPC failure, manual-review volume, and exception rooms, with DeBox group manual handling as the incident path.

BSC staging relayer evidence must archive safe RPC labels, nonce reservation, controlled SessionLocked and SessionSettled tx hashes, receipts, decoded events, and reconciliation results against approved BSC RPC.
Server must persist Room, Session, Round, Transcript, SettlementJob, AdminAudit, ready nonce, and action replay records through a database-backed repository boundary.
Developer App, App key/API Key, App Secret, HTTPS URL, bot/sender identity, optional endpoint-specific App ID if required, and open-access platform posture notes must be recorded.
Production wallet transaction capability and diagnostic typed-data signature method must be captured as DeBox App runtime evidence.
DeBox diagnostics must pass inside the DeBox app against an HTTPS test deployment with production-like credentials.
Share entry behavior must be documented and avoid exposing wallet addresses, balances, settlement ids, and secrets.
Initial fee rate must be 0.1% of net Session profit.
Production feeRateMax must be approved and documented before deployment.
Production fee treasury address must be approved and documented before deployment.
Fee changes must require announcement copy, at least 24h notice, an apply event, and must not affect active Sessions.
Production DeBox support group/contact must be configured and visible in settlement/manual-review states.

V1 classic operations policy must approve admin roles, owner/emergency address, relayer address, ordinary support authority, and trusted-relayer custody acceptance; multisig, formal compensation pool, player-authorization strategy, and heavier audit retention are post-v1 upgrades.
DeBox open-access platform posture is documented: no pre-review is assumed for technical launch; any later platform feedback is handled reactively while user-facing risk/support copy remains visible.

Before broad public real-money rooms are opened, owner/reviewer release approval must confirm P0/P1 remediation, dynamic browser money-flow evidence, release-gate posture, monitoring, support, and rollback runbook.
